unmapped.human_action: Search for corroborating signals: death doula training program enrollment data, INELDA membership trends, Google Trends for 'death doula training', any trade or wellness publication coverage independent of Dazed. Assess whether a vetting or credentialing platform already exists in the market.
unmapped.machine_action: Monitor death-positive, end-of-life, and death doula training keywords across wellness and culture RSS feeds. Flag any enrollment data, training provider announcements, insurance coverage developments, or search-volume spikes.
unmapped.methodology_footer: Signal sourced from Dazed Digital RSS cycle 2026-05-29. No enrollment data, no trade source, no search-volume data collected at time of drafting. Format selected as rich_people_beta on the basis of named high-status early adopters with no professional incentive to train.
unmapped.uncertainty: Primary uncertainty is source depth — one culture article with celebrity name-drops is not sector confirmation. The underlying demographic driver (peak boomer mortality) is real and measurable; the professional formation around it is the claim requiring verification.

original_issue.falsification: Thesis weakens if celebrity training turns out to be isolated personal development rather than a pattern; if death doula training enrollment shows no material growth in independent sources; or if the practitioner market is too thin to support a credentialing platform at viable unit economics.

signals: {'source': 'Dazed Digital RSS', 'url': 'https://www.dazeddigital.com/rss', 'quote': 'the number of people training to be death doulas has spiked, with celebrities like Nicole Kidman and Chloe Zhao joining their ranks — but why are so many of us being drawn to work with death?', 'date': '2026-05-29', 'type': 'editorial_mention'}
original_issue.thesis: Celebrity adoption of death doula training is the rich-people-beta signal for a profession about to be formalized — the near-term business is the credentialing and vetting infrastructure, not the doulas themselves.
original_issue.uncertainty: Primary uncertainty is source depth — one culture article with celebrity name-drops is not sector confirmation. The underlying demographic driver (peak boomer mortality) is real and measurable; the professional formation around it is the claim requiring verification.
